description_long: This is a rustic, cylindrical wooden bucket or small barrel, constructed from vertical wooden staves bound together by two dark metal hoops—one near the top rim and one near the base. The wood appears aged and weathered, with visible grain, knots, and some discoloration, suggesting prolonged outdoor exposure. The top rim is thick and rounded, with the interior open and hollow, showing the same rough wood texture as the exterior. The metal bands are slightly corroded, adding to the object's antique and utilitarian character. There are no visible handles or lid, and the object is slightly tapered, being wider at the top than at the bottom. One side shows faint, dark markings or stains, possibly remnants of old labeling or use.
description: A weathered, wooden bucket or small barrel with dark metal hoops and an open top, showing signs of age and outdoor use. The wood is rough and stained, and the object is slightly tapered with no handles or lid.
description_view_0: The front view shows the open top, thick rim, and clear wood grain with both metal hoops visible.
description_view_1: This angle highlights the uneven wood coloration and the pronounced taper from top to bottom.
description_view_2: The rear view is darker, with the wood appearing more shadowed and the metal bands less prominent.
description_view_3: This side reveals faint dark markings or stains on the wood, with the open top and both hoops visible.
attribute_banding_or_reinforcement: metal hoops at top and bottom
attribute_labeling_or_markings: faint dark markings on one side
